lesson.menu_desc: Participating: opinion phrases, agreeing and disagreeing -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 10 -- Meeting Basics -- Participating
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Aline Sberci | Aula 10 -- Meeting Basics

listenings.text: Hi Aline, this is Tom from the project team. I am calling about our team meeting tomorrow at ten. I just sent you the agenda, so please take a look before we start. We have three topics to discuss, and the most important one is the new deadline for the client. I would really like you to contribute your opinion, because you know the project very well. If you agree with the plan, that is great, and if you disagree, please say so. We need a clear decision by the end of the meeting. After that, someone will summarize the main points for the team. Thank you, and see you tomorrow.

This is a quick note to myself before the team meeting. First, I read the agenda, so I know the topics we will discuss. Then I prepare my opinion about the new deadline, because Tom asked me to contribute. In the meeting, I will share my idea in a clear and polite way. If I agree with a plan, I will say that is a good point. If I disagree, I will say I see your point, but I am not sure, and then I will suggest a better option. I want to ask one question to clarify the budget. At the end, I will help the team reach a decision and summarize the next steps. I feel ready to speak up.

extra_audio.text: First, Aline reads the agenda, so she knows the topics. Then she prepares her opinion about the deadline. After that, she shares her idea and asks one question to clarify the budget. Next, the team discusses the options and agrees on a plan. Finally, they reach a decision and Aline summarizes the next steps.
